title: How Long Does It Take to Make Money Wholesaling Real Estate?
excerpt: Most new wholesalers close their first deal within 30 to 90 days, but your timeline depends on your market, your hustle, and how well you understand the process. Here's a realistic breakdown of what to expect and how to speed things up.
author: AIWholesail Team
tags: how long does it take to make money wholesaling
metaDescription: How long does it take to make money wholesaling real estate? Most beginners close their first deal in 30-90 days. Learn what affects your timeline and how to speed it up.
metaKeywords: how long does it take to make money wholesaling, wholesaling real estate timeline, first wholesale deal, wholesaling for beginners, real estate wholesaling income, how fast can you make money wholesaling

sections.content: If you're thinking about getting into real estate wholesaling, one of the first questions you'll ask is: how long does it take to make money wholesaling? It's a fair question. You want to know if the time you invest will actually pay off. The honest answer is that most new wholesalers can close their first deal somewhere between 30 and 90 days of starting. Some do it faster. Some take six months. A small number give up before they ever collect a check. Your timeline depends on a handful of factors, and understanding them puts you in control.
The Realistic Timeline for Your First Wholesale Deal

Let's break the wholesaling process into its core stages. Each stage takes time, and knowing how long each one typically lasts helps you set realistic expectations and avoid discouragement when things don't happen overnight.

items: Weeks 1-2: Learning the basics — understanding ARV, MAO (Maximum Allowable Offer), contract assignments, and how to find motivated sellers
Weeks 2-4: Building your buyers list and identifying your target market
Weeks 3-6: Running your first marketing campaigns (direct mail, cold calling, driving for dollars, or online ads)
Weeks 4-8: Getting your first leads, making offers, and negotiating with sellers
Weeks 6-12: Locking up your first property under contract and assigning it to a cash buyer
Days after assignment: Collecting your assignment fee at closing, typically $5,000 to $20,000 per deal

sections.content: Notice that the timeline is front-loaded with learning and marketing. The money doesn't come until a deal closes, which is why many beginners underestimate how long the process takes. If you go in expecting a check in two weeks, you'll likely quit before you ever see one. If you budget 60 to 90 days for your first payday, you'll stay in the game long enough to win.
Key Factors That Affect How Quickly You Make Money
Your Market

High-activity markets with strong investor demand will speed up your timeline dramatically. In cities like Atlanta, Dallas, and Phoenix, cash buyers are actively hunting for deals and will move quickly when you bring them a good one. In slower markets, you might spend extra weeks just finding a buyer willing to close. If you're working in [Atlanta](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/atlanta-ga), [Dallas](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/dallas-tx), or [Phoenix](https://aiwholesail.com/markets/phoenix-az), you're already in territory where motivated sellers and active buyers overlap — a major advantage for your first deal.
Your Marketing Budget and Strategy

Wholesaling is not a zero-cost business. Even if you're starting lean, you need some marketing to generate leads. Cold calling and driving for dollars cost more time than money. Direct mail and pay-per-click ads cost more money than time. The more budget you put into lead generation, the faster the pipeline fills up. A wholesaler spending $1,000 a month on direct mail will typically close a deal faster than someone spending $0 and relying entirely on free methods.
How Quickly You Learn to Analyze Deals

One of the biggest reasons new wholesalers stall out is that they don't know how to run the numbers fast and confidently. If you're second-guessing every offer, you'll lose deals to faster investors. Learning to calculate ARV, estimate repair costs, and figure out your maximum allowable offer is not optional — it's the core skill. Use the free [Deal Calculator at aiwholesail.com](https://aiwholesail.com/tools/wholesale-deal-calculator) to run your numbers quickly and make offers with confidence. The faster you can analyze a deal, the faster you can lock one up.

Pro Tip: Don't wait until you have a deal under contract to build your buyers list. Start collecting cash buyer contacts from day one by attending local real estate investor meetups, posting in Facebook groups, and calling the 'We Buy Houses' signs you see around town. When you finally have a deal, you'll want to move fast — and that means having buyers ready before you need them.
What Does a Typical First Wholesale Deal Look Like?

Here's a simple example. A motivated seller in a mid-sized city has a distressed property with an ARV of $200,000. It needs about $40,000 in repairs. Using the standard formula — ARV x 70% minus repairs — a cash buyer would pay up to $100,000 for this property. You negotiate a purchase contract with the seller at $82,000. You assign that contract to your buyer for $90,000. Your assignment fee: $8,000. The whole process from lead to closing took 52 days. That's a realistic, repeatable scenario for a beginner who took the time to learn the fundamentals.

'Your first deal will take longer than you want and pay you less than you hoped — but it will teach you more than any course ever could. That's the deal that changes everything.' — Common wisdom among experienced wholesalers
How to Make Money Wholesaling Faster

Speed in wholesaling comes from focus. The investors who drag out their first deal are usually the ones who are still tweaking their marketing strategy, second-guessing their offers, or waiting for the perfect deal before they take action. Here's how to compress your timeline without cutting corners.

items: Pick one marketing channel and go deep on it — don't spread yourself thin across six methods
Make more offers — most beginners make too few, too cautiously. Aim for 5 to 10 offers per week once your pipeline is flowing
Use a CRM or even a simple spreadsheet to follow up with every lead consistently
Study your local market comp data until you can estimate ARV without a second thought
Join a local REIA (Real Estate Investors Association) to shortcut your buyers list and get mentorship
Partner with an experienced wholesaler on your first deal — take a smaller cut in exchange for real-world learning

sections.content: When Should You Expect Consistent Income?

Closing your first deal is a milestone, but it doesn't mean the money is now predictable. Most wholesalers need to close 3 to 5 deals before they start to understand their pipeline well enough to forecast income. If you're doing one deal a month at an average assignment fee of $8,000 to $12,000, you're looking at a six-figure business within a year. But getting to that point usually takes 6 to 12 months of consistent effort. If you want a deep dive into the full picture of how wholesaling fits into a broader investing strategy, check out our guide on [Real Estate Investing for Beginners](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/real-estate-investing-for-beginners).

If you're just getting started, the most valuable thing you can do right now is sharpen your fundamentals. Read our comprehensive [Wholesaling Real Estate for Beginners guide](https://aiwholesail.com/guides/wholesaling-real-estate-for-beginners) to understand the full process from finding deals to getting paid. Knowledge gaps are the number one reason new wholesalers take longer than necessary to close their first deal.

Pro Tip: Track every lead, every offer, and every follow-up from day one. Most wholesale deals don't close the first time you talk to a seller — they close on the third, fourth, or fifth follow-up weeks later. Investors who follow up consistently close 30% to 50% more deals than those who don't.
The Bottom Line

So, how long does it take to make money wholesaling? Plan for 30 to 90 days to close your first deal, 3 to 6 months to get comfortable with the process, and 6 to 12 months to build a consistent income stream. That's not slow — it's realistic. And compared to most businesses, getting paid $8,000 to $15,000 within your first 90 days with no license and no property purchase is genuinely impressive. The investors who succeed are the ones who treat it like a business from day one, stay consistent with their marketing, and never stop making offers.
